user_outcome: Public review and adjudication commands atomically persist, commit and push lifecycle transitions, recover without redispatch, and revalidate every S01–S13 bootstrap record before automation becomes authoritative.
rationale: This is the mandatory boundary between manually governed v0.15 bootstrap evidence and Sworn-owned automation. C-10 gives one stateful operation ownership of semantic identity, dispatch, append-only records, the local commit, the exact owner-track push, and restart recovery.
in_scope: Expose sworn maintainability review as the sole public current-authority review operation, accepting only Implementer preflight, Implementer closure, and fresh Verifier authoritative role-phase pairs; retain exact Baton prompt/schema bytes, compose the structured-output schema with the Sworn finding-disposition overlay, derive all semantic/lifecycle identity from committed authority, and inject the engine-owned provenance overlay before persistence. It has no planning-authority replacement edge. The exact v2 S02 replacement is executed earlier only by the section-4 manual bootstrap role adapter after a pushed immutable pre-dispatch claim; S13 revalidates that claim and result but never dispatches it. When C-04 has no included paths, persist the exact deterministic PASS report with zero model dispatch.

Expose sworn maintainability adjudicate as the sole public Coach transition through one typed public/internal request: Coach supplies decision, non-empty rationale and approved_by plus resume-only non-empty permitted touchpoints; committed terminal evidence derives eligible citation identities and byte-sorted unique blocking IDs, and the command captures approved_at once from its injected clock.

Require a clean starting tree and index and, for review/adjudication, validate C-03 through C-08 plus the operation-specific C-06 lifecycle before side effects; C-09 is not a review precondition and is evaluated only by the terminal cutover validator. Write the full report, compact status ledger, and journal transition coherently, create one decision-bearing commit, and push the exact owner track ref before protocol success; a valid FAIL is also committed and pushed before its non-zero result.

Recover a commit-without-push rerun by revalidating and reusing the exact committed report and transition with zero model dispatch, then complete only the missing exact-ref push; reject partial, rewritten, divergent, or caller-selected evidence.

Run an explicit engine-cutover validator over the committed S01–S13 specs, statuses, journals, reports, semantic identities, transitions, Git histories, the sealed v2 schema/manifest/claim-schema/receipt-schema digests, four ordered reconciliation entries, exact S01 live and S02 historical_report/live_review envelopes, 7696 actual-versus-expected index projection, permanent orphan declaration and invalid lifecycle/hold correction, the sole immutable replacement claim, continuity receipt and matching report disposition, and exact pinned Baton v0.15.1 Track Integrator commits for T1 then T2. Require equality among manifest declarations, claim token hash, receipt token/claim/report identity, report files, authoritative S01/S02 ledgers and exact orphan exclusions; this includes S13's independently verified planning-authority evidence and does not let S13, the stale index, or orphan PASS award authority. Refuse automated authority until every bootstrap artefact, tuple, envelope and integration passes unchanged.

After S13 is freshly verified and T1 then T2 have canonically merged to release-wt under the exact manual planning-authority Track Integrator transaction in planning/normative-clarifications.md section 4, expose the zero-dispatch C-13 sworn maintainability cutover <release> operation on that clean local/remote-equal release-wt before T5/T6 exist; revalidate every gate, change only protocol.json authority planning to current, commit and push once, and recover only an exact commit-without-push.

Provide structured fail-closed exit behavior and redacted diagnostics; expose no dry-run, no-push, force, waiver, lifecycle override, user-supplied fingerprint, report path, cycle, authority ref, or timestamp.
out_of_scope: Implementer, Verifier, router, and loop adapter adoption belongs to S14-role-lifecycle-recovery.
Track, release, ship, and re-plan adapter adoption belongs to S15-unified-track-merge through S17-engine-replan-migration.
The engine does not repair or synthesize any S01–S13 bootstrap report; a mismatch returns to the original slice authority.
The maintainability command does not merge, deploy, mark shipped, invent Coach decisions, or broaden permitted touchpoints.

effort_complexity.rationale: The public stateful operation owns model dispatch, immutable Git/report identity, multi-file commits, remote durability, exact recovery, and the self-bootstrap cutover.

risks.description: A crash between claim, model dispatch, report creation, status mutation, commit, and push creates ambiguous evidence or causes an expensive duplicate model call.
risks.mitigation: Keep the public command current-only; for the sole manual bootstrap replacement commit and push an immutable claim that consumes its dispatch slot before the model request, route claimed-but-unreported state to the Coach with zero redispatch, and allow ordinary restart reuse only when the complete committed transition exactly validates.

risks.description: The new engine certifies its own bootstrap by accepting records that differ from the manually governed evidence.
risks.mitigation: Cutover is read-only over S01–S13, including S13's separately verifier-certified evidence, and requires exact validation of live committed artefacts and histories; any mismatch is repaired and reverified under the original slice, never rewritten or self-certified by S13.

risks.description: A caller-controlled option bypasses Baton's role, phase, Coach authority, or durability budget, or the command infers a human decision field from model evidence.
risks.mitigation: Use one typed request separating Coach-supplied decision/rationale/identity/permissions from evidence-derived citations and command-captured time; reject missing, inferred, decision-incompatible, unknown, or override-like inputs before dispatch or mutation.

risks.description: Cutover preserves or promotes the planning-only v2 reconciliation, stale index, orphan PASS, or replacement permission after automated authority activates.
risks.mitigation: Independently reconstruct both digests, every live/historical envelope, merge/path/projection tuple, authoritative ledger, orphan denial and replacement disposition; require exact declaration/file/ledger equality, reject extension or orphan promotion, and retire all v2 recognition when authority changes to current.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary runs sworn maintainability review under current authority for an eligible Implementer preflight or closure or fresh Verifier authoritative phase on a clean owner-track worktree THE SYSTEM SHALL derive C-03 through C-08 inputs from committed state, recompute scope and a new invocation, dispatch the exact Baton prompt with the composed finding-disposition constraint, validate and inject provenance, atomically write report/status/journal, commit and push the exact track ref, and return success only for PASS; planning authority, the v2 orphan, replacement authorization, or replacement claim SHALL be rejected before this handler (C-10, N-04, N-05, N-09, N-10).

WHEN the same command receives a schema-valid maintainability FAIL THE SYSTEM SHALL append its blocking finding identities, commit and push the coherent transition before returning non-zero, SHALL NOT convert it to BLOCKED, and SHALL enforce the exact C-06 needs_coach or re_slice_required next state for the current cycle (C-05, C-06, C-10, N-05).

WHEN a review transition commit exists locally but its exact owner-track push previously failed THE SYSTEM SHALL validate and reuse that committed report and transition, perform zero model dispatches, push only that commit to the expected ref, and produce the same protocol result; IF any report, status, journal, ref, or blob identity differs THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL fail before push (C-05, C-10, N-04, N-10).

WHEN the built binary runs sworn maintainability adjudicate for an eligible cycle-0 terminal failure and the Coach supplies decision, non-empty rationale and approved_by plus resume-only non-empty unique permitted touchpoints from spec.touchpoints THE SYSTEM SHALL derive chronological citation IDs/fingerprints and the byte-sorted unique blocking-ID union from committed evidence, capture approved_at once, and commit/push the exact immutable C-06 adjudication; re_slice SHALL forbid permitted touchpoints and SHALL NOT invent a rollback slice or replacement scope (C-06, C-10, N-05).

IF current-authority review receives an invalid role-phase pair, exhausted dispatch budget, dirty tree/index, stale/divergent ref, invalid protocol/report/lifecycle/fingerprint/overlay, any planning/v2 orphan/replacement/claim authority, or any dry-run, no-push, force, waiver, lifecycle-override, caller-selected-cycle/report/authority option, OR adjudication omits Coach rationale/identity/resume permissions, supplies derived citation/timestamp fields, or supplies decision-incompatible permissions THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero with zero model dispatches and leave refs, index, worktree, reports, status, and journal unchanged; the manual bootstrap adapter separately routes any invalid or claimed-but-unreported replacement to the Coach (C-03, C-10, N-05, N-09).

WHEN the S13 cutover validator runs against committed release authority THE SYSTEM SHALL revalidate every S01–S13 spec/status/report/blob/fingerprint/lifecycle/evidence/rollback/protocol/integration fact and reproduce the duplicate-key-free v2 schema, manifest, replacement-claim schema and continuity-receipt schema digests, four ordered entries, eight exceptional historical release-record paths plus the separate claim and receipt paths, exact S01 live and S02 historical_report/live_review envelopes, 7696 actual blob 998b44c0 plus independently rendered expected blob 9bf11cda/SHA/size/ref snapshot, orphan f4ef4f75 report/three commits, lifecycle status blob 83f33160, hold status blob 4f0b59e6 and every declared forensic/authority flag, the exact invalid-lifecycle prefix correction, and the sole pushed immutable replacement claim plus token-matching receipt/report disposition. It SHALL require exact equality among declarations, claim hash, receipt token/claim/report identity, report files, authoritative ledgers and orphan exclusions without mutation or awarding S13, the stale index or orphan PASS authority; any mismatch SHALL block automated authority and identify the owning original slice (C-03 through C-10, N-09, N-10).

WHEN S01 through S13 are freshly verified, T1 then T2 have exact pinned Baton v0.15.1 two-parent Track Integrator commits on clean local/remote-equal release-wt, no T5/T6 ref exists, every planning, ambiguity, engine and evidence gate passes, and the validator has independently reconstructed exact v2 schema/manifest/claim-schema/receipt-schema/declaration/file/ledger equality including the non-authoritative 7696 index and orphan, exact hold correction, and consumed one-shot claim with its token-matching receipt and replacement result THE SYSTEM SHALL let sworn maintainability cutover <release> recognize the two manual integrations, retire every planning-only v2 exception and replacement permission, change only protocol.json authority planning to current, commit/push once, and return 0; missing or unreported claim, missing receipt, token/claim/report mismatch, second claim/replacement, orphan promotion, authorization widening, manifest extension, mismatch, bootstrap divergence, downgrade, force, alternate ref or premature activation SHALL exit non-zero without mutation (C-03, C-13, N-09, N-10).

WHEN sworn maintainability review receives an eligible exact C-04 scope with no included paths THE SYSTEM SHALL persist and durably transition the deterministic schema-valid PASS report for that empty manifest identity, perform zero model dispatches, and expose the same PASS/exit result and restart identity as any other authorized PASS (C-04, C-05, C-10, N-03, N-10).
